A truly monumental, incredibly historic, and massive geopolitical triumph was aggressively achieved in Washington, D.C. In Andhra Pradesh, Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu aggressively capitalized on Prime Minister Vajpayee's incredibly massive, highly successful diplomatic tour of the United States. ## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On September 14, 1901, US President William McKinley officially and tragically passed away from massive gangrene infections caused by the horrific assassination wounds he sustained eight days prior. His massive, sudden death aggressively catapulted Vice President Theodore Roosevelt, an incredibly dynamic, massively progressive, and fiercely imperialistic political force, into the absolute highest office of the United States, fundamentally and aggressively altering the massive trajectory of the American executive branch and global geopolitical projection for the 20th century.

On September 14, 1960, the incredibly massive, highly cartelized, and globally defining Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) was officially and aggressively founded at the Baghdad Conference. Initiated heavily by Iran, Iraq, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, and Venezuela, this massive intergovernmental organization was fiercely designed to absolutely break the massive, monopolistic grip of Western multinational oil companies (the "Seven Sisters") and aggressively secure absolute, sovereign control over global petroleum production and incredibly massive national pricing strategies.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On September 14, 1949, the incredibly massive, fiercely debated, and deeply historic Constituent Assembly of India officially and aggressively adopted Hindi written in the Devanagari script as the absolute official language of the Republic of India. This massive, monumental linguistic decision, aggressively balancing the incredibly diverse, massive linguistic heritage of the subcontinent, heavily established the foundational administrative architecture of the nation, and is fiercely and proudly celebrated annually across the republic as massive Hindi Diwas.

On September 14, 1839, Lord William Bentinck, the incredibly massive, highly reforming, and deeply influential former Governor-General of India, officially passed away in Paris. His incredibly massive, aggressively progressive tenure was heavily defined by his absolute, monumental collaboration with Raja Ram Mohan Roy to officially, legally, and aggressively abolish the horrific, massive practice of *Sati* (widow immolation) in 1829, fundamentally establishing his legacy as a massive, defining architect of sweeping social reforms during the British colonial era.
